MINI MINUTES FOR NOV-18-25 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF GABRIEL ESPINOZA'S STIPULATED MOTION TO FILE CONSOLIDATED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. GOOD CAUSE APPEARING, THE COURT ORDERS THE CAPTIONED CASES CONSOLIDATED FOR ALL PURPOSES AND GRANTS MOVING PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR LEAVE TO FILE A CONSOLIDATED COMPLAINT. MOVING PLAINTIFF MUST FILE HIS CONSOLIDATED COMPLAINT, SUBSTANTIALLY IDENTICAL TO THE ATTACHMENT TO THE STIPULATION FORTHWITH BUT NO LATER THAN DECEMBER 2, 2025. THE LEAD CASE IS CGC-25-621956. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/JMQ)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-13-2026 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
Off Calendar Apr-07-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiffs Gabriel Espinoza and Ema Bell's Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment is GRANTED.

Good cause appearing, Plaintiff's motion to approve settlement pursuant to Health and Safety Code section 25249.7(f) and Code of Civil Procedure section 664.6 is granted. Based on the record before it, the court finds: (1) the injunctive relief required by the settlement complies with Proposition 65; (2) the penalty amounts are reasonable based on the criteria set forth in Health and Safety Code section 25249.7(b)(2); and, (3) the award of attorney's fees is reasonable under California law. The consent judgments shall be entered consistent with the Proposition 65 Settlement and the Parties' [Proposed] Consent Judgment.

Prior to the hearing time, Moving Party shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed order quoting the text above verbatim.

Prior to the hearing time, Moving Party must lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a copy of the proposed consent judgment executed by all relevant parties.

Order and Consent Judgment are signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-29-2026 Continued To Jun-10-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-29-2025 Continued To Feb-11-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Consolidated Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-18-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF GABRIEL ESPINOZA's Motion To File Consolidated Complaint.

Plaintiff Gabriel Espinoza's stipulated Motion To File Consolidated Complaint is GRANTED.

Good cause appearing, the court orders the captioned cases consolidated for all purposes and grants Moving Plaintiff's request for leave to file a consolidated complaint. Moving Plaintiff must file his consolidated complaint, substantially identical to the attachment to the stipulation forthwith but no later than December 2, 2025. The lead case is CGC-25-621956.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
